question.content: For the boatman-canoe isolated system, state the final canoe displacement direction and final velocity state after the boatman sits at the front. Output exactly one ASCII line in the format x_f<x_i;v_f->0, using semicolons as separators, no Chinese characters, no prose sentence, and no extra text.

step_1.content: Take boatman plus canoe as an isolated horizontal system in water without drag.
step_1.key_point: No external horizontal force, so center-of-mass position is invariant.

step_3.content: When the boatman moves forward inside the canoe, the canoe recoils backward to keep the same center of mass.
step_3.key_point: Forward internal motion of m induces backward displacement of the canoe.

step_6.content: After he sits down at the front, no internal relative motion remains, so the final state is static.
step_6.key_point: End of internal motion implies final velocity is zero.
